title: gouldia kapuaensis var. pittosporoides
intro: Gouldia kapuaensis var. pittosporoides, also known as the Hawaiian gouldia, is a rare and endangered plant species native to the island of Kaua'i in Hawaii. It is a type of flowering plant that belongs to the family Myrsinaceae and has been used in traditional Hawaiian medicine for its medicinal properties. The plant has been found to possess antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which contribute to its potential health benefits, including wound healing, reduction of fever, and treatment of various infections. In traditional Hawaiian medicine, the leaves and stems of the plant are prepared as infusions or teas for their medicinal properties, and it is also used in herbal preparations such as salves and poultices to treat various health conditions.
